name: A SUPERIOR PROPERTY MANAGEMENT
address: 225 NW FRANKLIN SUITE A, BEND, OR 97703

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Roof System; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Improving the accessibility of the property for emergency responders could be achieved by considering enhancements to the current access point, ensuring it is sufficiently wide and clearly marked. By optimizing signage and visibility of entry paths, responders may find navigation and movement through the property more efficient. The designation of a vehicle staging area that avoids interfering with building access or pedestrian walkways may also facilitate smoother operations. To support coordinated response efforts, maintaining clear and unobstructed pedestrian paths would aid in rapid personnel movement between multiple structures. Additionally, ensuring that landscaped areas do not impede sightlines or block building visibility from approaches could assist responders in quickly locating key operational zones upon arrival.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Trees
wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space and reducing fuel continuity around the property can be achieved by strategically managing the tree cluster and the vegetation surrounding the structure. Creating separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation could help to minimize structure exposure. Enhancing the clear separation between scattered trees will also reduce opportunities for fire spread. Treating the vegetated perimeter by creating breaks or increasing spacing between vegetation may further contribute to fuel continuity reduction. Maintaining open areas and ensuring vegetation is regularly managed can support long-term resilience against wildfires. These actions would not only strengthen the property’s preparation but also facilitate efficient firefighter access and suppression capabilities.
